system_description.overview: Velostics Inc is a logistics SaaS platform automating inbound/outbound processes for shippers, ranging from managed transportation to yard management leveraging AI. The platform includes appointment scheduling, dock management, yard management, network visibility, and AI agent services.

products.Slot: Appointment scheduling portal for shippers to allow brokers/carriers to book slots
products.Dock: Slot plus gate check-in (Pass) and advanced dock scheduling for plants and warehouses
products.Yard: Dock plus full Google Maps-based yard management system, jockey task management, trailer management
products.Network: Appointment dashboard for brokers/carriers/schedulers; shared calendars for suppliers and consignees
products.AI Agents: Suite of AI Agent services including Voice AI, Vision AI, and Answers AI

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Oracle Cloud (carve-out — Oracle provides data center services; Velostics controls application/service layer)
regions: Not specified — Oracle high-availability data centers with multiple availability zones
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices or servers; all cloud
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (Oracle high-availability data centers with multiple availability zones)
infrastructure.cdn: Cloudflare (visible in network diagram as first layer before Auth0)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in Oracle VPC with segmented VPCs and ACLs. Public network contains Kubernetes Cluster; private networks contain PostgreSQL DB and Redis (each behind NAT gateways). Production servers maintain failover capabilities.
network_architecture.waf: Cloudflare (implied — sits in front of Auth0 and application)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via Oracle (employed through Oracle VPC security policy network settings)
network_architecture.vpn: VPN via bastion host for remote access
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) using third-party CA certificates for all web-based traffic
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; systems restricted to internet via content filtering
network_architecture.topology: User -> Cloudflare -> Auth0 -> Internet Gateway -> Oracle Cloud VPC (Kubernetes Cluster in public network) -> NAT Gateway DB -> PostgreSQL (private) / NAT Gateway Redis -> Redis (private). GitHub CI/CD feeds Oracle Container Registry -> Kubernetes.

application_architecture.pattern: Microservices (Kubernetes-orchestrated with separate services: Clockwork, Sidekiq, Web visible in diagram)
application_architecture.environments: Separate Stage and Production environments; changes tested in Stage before Production

data_storage.backup_frequency: Formal backup policy in place; frequency not specified

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Auth0 (visible in network diagram between Cloudflare and Oracle Cloud)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code tool access
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Passwords controlled through Oracle policy — periodic forced changes, expiry, complexity requirements; accounts locked after failed attempts; guest/anonymous logins not allowed

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (Oracle KMS) — 'organization uses its cloud provider key management service to encrypt data at rest'
encryption.key_management: Oracle KMS — 'access to production environment's access keys is restricted to authorized individuals'

ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured — every merge request to move change to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot make changes to production without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configurations with rollback capability (specific tool not named)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management set up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations receive automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance; critical/security OS patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Internal monitoring tool (specific product not named); IT team monitors system capacity and performance, generates alerts at predefined thresholds
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configurations, permissions, roles at application and infrastructure level
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events; incident management process invoked for confirmed events

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure — data center, VPC, compute, storage, KMS
CDN / edge security / WAF (first hop in network diagram)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software on all production servers (continuous scanning, weekly definition updates) and Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan before every production release
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center facilities delegated to Oracle (carve-out); Oracle responsible for physical security
A1.2 — Availability/environmental controls managed by Oracle/building management; Velostics has no physical office
Processing Integrity — determined not relevant to system
Privacy — determined not relevant to system
notable_controls: CC6.2.2 — Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged users
CC6.1.4 — Access revoked within 1 business day upon termination
CC8.1.2 — Branch protection: every merge to production requires additional approval
CC4.1.1 — Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC7.4.1 — BCDR plan documented, reviewed annually and tested annually across loss scenarios

infrastructure.rationale: Oracle Cloud with Kubernetes, multi-AZ failover, Cloudflare CDN, VPC with network segmentation. However single cloud provider (Oracle, unusual choice), no IaC tool named, no multi-region. Solid mid-tier.

application_architecture.rationale: Microservices on Kubernetes with separate components (Clockwork/Sidekiq/Web), branch protection, code review required, separate environments. Languages/frameworks not disclosed.

data_layer.rationale: PostgreSQL + Redis is a clean stack, multi-AZ, per-tenant segregation, data classification policy, retention policy. No data warehouse mentioned, backup frequency unspecified, file storage not disclosed.

security_posture.rationale: Strong: Cloudflare WAF, IDS/IPS via Oracle, quarterly access reviews, 1-day access revocation, monthly vuln scans, annual external pen test, MFA on admin/source code, antivirus on all systems. Missing: SIEM named, no cyber insurance confirmed.

devops_maturity.rationale: GitHub + GitHub CI/CD, Kubernetes orchestration, separate environments, branch protection with approval gates, monthly patch reviews, automated workstation patching. IaC tool unnamed, no named monitoring product.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, annual review and annual testing, multi-AZ failover in Oracle. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region DR, backup frequency unspecified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: 4 identifiable vendors: Oracle Cloud (critical), Cloudflare, Auth0, GitHub. Heavy Oracle dependency is unusual and creates concentration risk — Oracle is not mainstream AWS/Azure/GCP with the same breadth of managed services.

overall.rationale: Solid logistics SaaS with a well-architected Oracle Cloud deployment, Kubernetes microservices, strong access controls, and annual pen testing. Main concerns: Oracle Cloud concentration risk (non-mainstream provider), no RTO/RPO disclosed, IaC and monitoring tools unnamed, and no multi-region DR.

red_flags: Oracle Cloud is an unconventional primary cloud provider — limited ecosystem, smaller talent pool, potential migration risk
No RTO or RPO disclosed for business continuity
yellow_flags: IaC tool not named — 'configuration management tool' is vague
Backup frequency not specified despite formal backup policy
No named APM/SIEM tool — monitoring described generically
No multi-region failover — single-region with multi-AZ only
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded — may be expected for logistics platform but worth noting
green_flags: Network diagram clearly shows Cloudflare + Auth0 + Kubernetes architecture — unusually detailed and transparent
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
1-business-day access revocation SLA — strong offboarding control
Branch protection with mandatory approval for all production merges
Monthly vulnerability scanning on infrastructure and applications
Multi-AZ Oracle infrastructure with failover capabilities
BCDR plan tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
MFA enforced for admin access and source code repository access
key_observations: Velostics is unusual in using Oracle Cloud as primary infrastructure — likely chosen for Oracle's logistics/database heritage, but creates vendor concentration risk that AWS/GCP/Azure customers would not face

The network diagram (p.18) is exceptionally detailed for a SOC 2 report, clearly showing Cloudflare -> Auth0 -> Kubernetes flow with PostgreSQL and Redis in private subnets — indicates mature architecture thinking
Sidekiq in the architecture suggests Ruby/Rails background stack (Sidekiq is a Ruby background job processor), though languages are not explicitly disclosed
Clockwork in the architecture is a Ruby scheduling library — reinforces Ruby/Rails inference
The AI Agents product line (Voice AI, Vision AI, Answers AI) is not architecturally detailed — likely newer additions with unclear infrastructure
Oracle is listed as the sole subservice organization with a SOC 2 report of its own — carve-out method used cleanly
CC6.4 (physical access) and A1.2 (environmental) are explicitly excluded as Oracle's responsibility — appropriate carve-out
